UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information in the application. This entity stores non-authentication related user data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and account identification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 AuthenticationSystemUser)

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single Olipop drink flavor variant available for sale, including its visual and descriptive data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity (e.g., 'guava-soda').

name.description: The primary name of the drink flavor (e.g., 'Guava').

subtitle.description: A secondary descriptor for the product (e.g., 'Soda').

description.description: A captivating, AI-generated short narrative description for the flavor.

displayColorName.description: The descriptive color name associated with the flavor for UI differentiation (e.g., 'Green', 'Deep Red').

price.description: The selling price of one unit of the product.

stockQuantity.description: The number of units of this product currently available in stock.

imageSequenceBasePath.description: The base path to the directory containing the WebP image sequence for this product (e.g., '/guava/').

imageSequenceFrameCount.description: The total number of frames in the WebP image sequence for this product (e.g., 240).

framesId.description: A unique identifier for the specific video frame sequence (e.g., 'WA0022').

nutritionFactsUrl.description: A URL to an image or document detailing the product's nutrition facts.

ingredients.description: A list of key ingredients in the drink.

benefits.description: A list of health or functional benefits associated with the drink.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product entry was last updated.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's shopping cart, containing items they intend to purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this cart. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product with a specific quantity within a user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being added to the cart. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart. Must be at least 1.

priceAtAddToCart.description: The price of the product at the moment it was added to the cart, for historical accuracy.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed purchase made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and applicable taxes/shipping.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processed', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

shippingAddress.description: The full shipping address for the order.

billingAddress.description: The full billing address for the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product with its purchased quantity and price within a specific order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product purchased in this order item. Must be at least 1.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of purchase.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a user's review and rating for a specific product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product being reviewed. (Relationship: Product 1:N Review)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted the review. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Review)

rating.description: The rating given by the user, typically an integer between 1 and 5.

comment.description: The textual feedback or comment provided by the user in their review.

reviewDate.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the review record was last updated.

FAQItem.title: FAQItem
FAQItem.description: Represents a single Frequently Asked Question and its answer, to be displayed in an accordion layout.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FAQItem entity.

question.description: The question text of the FAQ.

answer.description: The answer text corresponding to the FAQ question.

category.description: An optional category to group related FAQs (e.g., 'Shipping', 'Product Info').

displayOrder.description: An integer to define the display order of the FAQ item within its category or globally.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the FAQ item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the FAQ item was last updated.

definition.description: Stores a user's private profile information. Each document's ID is the user's UID. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Contains public details for each Olipop product variant. Publicly readable. Write operations are restricted to authenticated administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product (e.g., 'guava-soda').

definition.description: Represents a user's single shopping cart, identified by the fixed document ID 'cart' under their user path. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userId` in the path. The document's `userId` field should match the path's `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Items within a user's shopping cart. Access is restricted to the authenticated user owning the parent cart. The parent `userId` is implicitly denormalized through the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item in the cart.

definition.description: A completed purchase order by a user. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userId` in the path. The document's `userId` field should match the path's `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific order.

definition.description: Individual items within a user's specific order. Access is restricted to the authenticated user owning the parent order. The parent `userId` and `orderId` are implicitly denormalized through the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item within an order.

definition.description: User reviews for a specific product. Publicly readable. Includes denormalized 'userId' within the document for write authorization (only the author can modify/delete their own review), ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific review.

definition.description: General Frequently Asked Questions. Publicly readable. Write operations are restricted to authenticated administrators.

params.name: faqId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific FAQ item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES, prioritizing Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling. Each collection path is chosen to simplify security rules, ensure data integrity, and support scalable query patterns.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design achieves authorization independence primarily through:
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For user-specific and user-owned data (UserProfile, Cart, CartItem, Order, OrderItem), data is structured under `/users/{userId}/...`. This means `request.auth.uid` can be directly compared against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to verify ownership. For example, a user can only read/write documents at `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/profile` or `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/cart/items/{itemId}`.
2.  **Denormalization:** For the `Review` entity, which is product-centric but user-authored, the `userId` of the reviewer is denormalized and stored directly within the `Review` document itself (`/products/{productId}/reviews/{reviewId}`). This allows rules to check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` for update/delete operations without performing a `get()` call on any parent document or user profile.
3.  **Existence-Based Global Roles:** Although not explicitly listed in `firestorePaths` due to schema constraints (as no `AdminRole` entity schema was provided), administrative roles for managing public data (like `Product` and `FAQItem`) would typically be implemented using an existence-based check, e.g., `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`. This check does not depend on data within the target document collection, further ensuring independence.

**Support for QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
Query as Authorization Predicates (QAPs) are supported by ensuring that `list` operations are inherently secure:
1.  **Public Collections (`/products`, `/faqs`):** These collections are designed for public read access. Any `list` query on these collections will return data that is authorized for all users, thus no filtering is required by the rules. For example, `db.collection('products').where('price', '<', 10)` is secure.
2.  **User-Owned Subcollections (`/users/{userId}/cart/items`, `/users/{userId}/orders`):** For these collections, the `list` operation is secure because the parent path (`/users/{userId}`) inherently restricts the query to data owned by the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`). A query like `db.collection('users/$(request.auth.uid)/orders').where('status', '==', 'pending')` is secure and will only return the user's own pending orders.
3.  **Product-Centric Reviews (`/products/{productId}/reviews`):** Reviews are publicly readable. `list` operations on this subcollection (e.g., `db.collection('products/guava-soda/reviews').orderBy('rating', 'desc')`) are secure as all reviews are authorized for public view.

**Structural Segregation:**
Each collection or subcollection is designed to hold documents with a homogeneous security posture. For example, `/products` contains only publicly viewable products, and `/users/{userId}/cart/items` contains only cart items private to that user. There is no mixing of public/private or admin/user-only data within the same collection, which greatly simplifies security rules.
